title: Mission Brief
description: Formation Flight dispatch payload: the typed coherence contract an orchestrator (Tower) hands DOWN to one parallel agent (a sortie) before it flies its seam. It is the structural fix for formation breakup — the 'don't do X' constraints that otherwise evaporate at turn 47 travel WITH the agent into isolation. Canonical spec: SPEC-INTENT-MISSION-BRIEF-001. Source of truth for the MissionBrief dataclass (formation/formation.py) — that dataclass is test-pinned to this schema.

brief_id.description: Unique id for this brief. Convention: MB-<ulid> (ULID per SPEC-003). One brief per seam per formation run.

intent.description: Parent intent id (INT-...) the whole formation is flying. The shared 'why'. All briefs in one formation share this — it is the shared reference frame anchor and the ON-trigger key (>=2 agents on the same intent => formation).

seam_id.description: The interface boundary this agent owns. Unique within the formation; two briefs NEVER share a seam_id (separation minima — no two aircraft in the same airspace). Decomposition is on seams, not files (SPEC-INTENT-SEAM-DECOMPOSITION-001).

interface_contract.description: Contract id (CON-...) — the FROZEN seam. Work-Ontology level 4. The agent MUST NOT change it unilaterally; changing it is a contract breach the coherence gate flags. References the existing Contract entity (ContractType.INTERFACE, servers/models.py); does not redefine it.

reference_frame.description: The shared datum every aircraft navigates against. Without it, 'straight and level' means something different to each agent — the root cause of vocabulary drift.

glossary.description: Canonical term -> definition. The agent must use these terms with these meanings.

canonical_terms.description: Terms that MUST appear verbatim (e.g. 'Mission Brief', not a synonym).

forbidden_synonyms.description: Canonical term -> [synonyms whose appearance signals drift]. Coherence-gate Stage A flags any forbidden synonym in a Mission Report's vocabulary_terms.

invariants.description: Things that must remain true for this seam. The constraints that evaporate at turn 47. Checked by coherence-gate Stage A against the Mission Report's invariants_selfcheck and (where testable) the verification_command.

non_goals.description: Explicit out-of-scope. No existing Intent artifact carries this concept — the Mission Brief is its home (and the reason audit_chain alone cannot catch non-goal violation).

drift_markers.description: What counts as drift for THIS seam (beyond the global checks): specific files not to touch, decisions not to revisit, framings not to re-open. Feeds the loop-until-drift-clean stop condition.

trust_gate.description: Autonomy band for this seam (Intent trust model, trust_to_autonomy). Sets the privilege ceiling. The formation governance matrix (SPEC-INTENT-FORMATION-GOVERNANCE-001) maps trust_gate x lambda -> (model, isolation, privilege, verification intensity).

lambda.description: Coefficient of bravery for this seam. References the product's .intent/INTENT.md lambda_settings (autonomy-flight-model §16). High-lambda solo surfaces earn worktree+Opus+adversarial-verify; cross-human stays 0.

isolation.description: Physical containment (Lift, the force that buys autonomy). 'worktree' => own git worktree (parallel mutate, no file collision); 'readonly' => no mutate; 'none' => shared workspace (only safe at N=1 or for non-overlapping reads).

model.description: Model tier for this sortie. Assigned by the governance matrix from trust_gate x lambda. Omit to inherit the orchestrator's model.

verification_rubric.description: How this seam's Contract is checked. References the Contract's verification_command — it does NOT redefine a parallel rubric. Coherence-gate Stage A actually RUNS this command (audit_chain only checks that a verified: flag exists).

verification_command.description: Shell command that must exit 0 for the seam to be airworthy.

lineage.description: OTel trace context. Reuses make_event/TraceContext (servers/models.py) — does not mint a new lineage scheme. This is how the formation is observable through the existing event fabric and how the Observe loop closes.
